fields.name: PE
fields.description: Peripheral enable Note: When PE = 0, the I2C SCL and SDA lines are released. Internal state machines and status bits are put back to their reset value. When cleared, PE must be kept low for at least 3 APB clock cycles.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Peripheral disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Peripheral enabled

fields.name: TXIE
fields.description: TX Interrupt enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Transmit (TXIS) interrupt disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Transmit (TXIS) interrupt enabled

fields.name: RXIE
fields.description: RX Interrupt enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Receive (RXNE) interrupt disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Receive (RXNE) interrupt enabled

fields.name: ADDRIE
fields.description: Address match Interrupt enable (slave only)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Address match (ADDR) interrupts disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Address match (ADDR) interrupts enabled

fields.name: NACKIE
fields.description: Not acknowledge received Interrupt enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Not acknowledge (NACKF) received interrupts disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Not acknowledge (NACKF) received interrupts enabled

fields.name: STOPIE
fields.description: Stop detection Interrupt enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Stop detection (STOPF) interrupt disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Stop detection (STOPF) interrupt enabled

fields.name: TCIE
fields.description: Transfer Complete interrupt enable Note: Any of these events generate an interrupt: Note: Transfer Complete (TC) Note: Transfer Complete Reload (TCR)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Transfer Complete interrupt disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Transfer Complete interrupt enabled

fields.name: ERRIE
fields.description: Error interrupts enable Note: Any of these errors generate an interrupt: Note: Arbitration Loss (ARLO) Note: Bus Error detection (BERR) Note: Overrun/Underrun (OVR) Note: Timeout detection (TIMEOUT) Note: PEC error detection (PECERR) Note: Alert pin event detection (ALERT)

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Error detection interrupts disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Error detection interrupts enabled

fields.name: DNF
fields.description: Digital noise filter These bits are used to configure the digital noise filter on SDA and SCL input. The digital filter, filters spikes with a length of up to DNF[3:0] * tsubI2CCLK/sub sub.../sub Note: If the analog filter is also enabled, the digital filter is added to the analog filter. Note: This filter can only be programmed when the I2C is disabled (PE = 0).

enum_values.name: NoFilter
enum_values.description: Digital filter disabled

enum_values.name: Filter1
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 1 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter2
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 2 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter3
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 3 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter4
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 4 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter5
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 5 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter6
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 6 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter7
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 7 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter8
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 8 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter9
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 9 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter10
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 10 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter11
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 11 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter12
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 12 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter13
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 13 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter14
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 14 tI2CCLK

enum_values.name: Filter15
enum_values.description: Digital filter enabled and filtering capability up to 15 tI2CCLK

fields.name: ANFOFF
fields.description: Analog noise filter OFF Note: This bit can only be programmed when the I2C is disabled (PE = 0).

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Analog noise filter enabled

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Analog noise filter disabled

fields.name: TXDMAEN
fields.description: DMA transmission requests enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: DMA mode disabled for transmission

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: DMA mode enabled for transmission

fields.name: RXDMAEN
fields.description: DMA reception requests enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: DMA mode disabled for reception

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: DMA mode enabled for reception

fields.name: SBC
fields.description: Slave byte control This bit is used to enable hardware byte control in slave mode.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Slave byte control disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Slave byte control enabled

fields.name: NOSTRETCH
fields.description: Clock stretching disable This bit is used to disable clock stretching in slave mode. It must be kept cleared in master mode. Note: This bit can only be programmed when the I2C is disabled (PE = 0).

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Clock stretching enabled

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Clock stretching disabled

fields.name: WUPEN
fields.description: Wakeup from Stop mode enable Note: If the Wakeup from Stop mode feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation. Note: WUPEN can be set only when DNF = '0000'

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Wakeup from Stop mode disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Wakeup from Stop mode enabled

fields.name: GCEN
fields.description: General call enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: General call disabled. Address 0b00000000 is NACKed

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: General call enabled. Address 0b00000000 is ACKed

fields.name: SMBHEN
fields.description: SMBus host address enable Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Host address disabled. Address 0b0001000x is NACKed

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Host address enabled. Address 0b0001000x is ACKed

fields.name: SMBDEN
fields.description: SMBus device default address enable Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Device default address disabled. Address 0b1100001x is NACKed

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Device default address enabled. Address 0b1100001x is ACKed

fields.name: ALERTEN
fields.description: SMBus alert enable Note: When ALERTEN=0, the SMBA pin can be used as a standard GPIO. Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: In device mode (SMBHEN=Disabled) Releases SMBA pin high and Alert Response Address Header disabled (0001100x) followed by NACK. In host mode (SMBHEN=Enabled) SMBus Alert pin (SMBA) not supported

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: In device mode (SMBHEN=Disabled) Drives SMBA pin low and Alert Response Address Header enabled (0001100x) followed by ACK.In host mode (SMBHEN=Enabled) SMBus Alert pin (SMBA) supported

fields.name: PECEN
fields.description: PEC enable Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: PEC calculation disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: PEC calculation enabled

fields.name: FMP
fields.description: Fast-mode Plus 20 mA drive enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: 20 mA I/O drive disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: 20 mA I/O drive enabled

fields.name: ADDRACLR
fields.description: Address match flag (ADDR) automatic clear

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: ADDR flag is set by hardware, cleared by software

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: ADDR flag remains cleared by hardware

fields.name: STOPFACLR
fields.description: STOP detection flag (STOPF) automatic clear

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: STOPF flag is set by hardware, cleared by software

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: STOPF flag remains cleared by hardware

fields.name: SADD
fields.description: Slave address (master mode) In 7-bit addressing mode (ADD10 = 0): SADD[7:1] should be written with the 7-bit slave address to be sent. The bits SADD[9], SADD[8] and SADD[0] are don't care. In 10-bit addressing mode (ADD10 = 1): SADD[9:0] should be written with the 10-bit slave address to be sent. Note: Changing these bits when the START bit is set is not allowed.

fields.name: RD_WRN
fields.description: Transfer direction (master mode) Note: Changing this bit when the START bit is set is not allowed.

enum_values.name: Write
enum_values.description: Master requests a write transfer

enum_values.name: Read
enum_values.description: Master requests a read transfer

fields.name: ADD10
fields.description: 10-bit addressing mode (master mode) Note: Changing this bit when the START bit is set is not allowed.

enum_values.name: Bit7
enum_values.description: The master operates in 7-bit addressing mode

enum_values.name: Bit10
enum_values.description: The master operates in 10-bit addressing mode

fields.name: HEAD10R
fields.description: 10-bit address header only read direction (master receiver mode) Note: Changing this bit when the START bit is set is not allowed.

enum_values.name: Complete
enum_values.description: The master sends the complete 10 bit slave address read sequence

enum_values.name: Partial
enum_values.description: The master only sends the 1st 7 bits of the 10 bit address, followed by Read direction

fields.name: START
fields.description: Start generation This bit is set by software, and cleared by hardware after the Start followed by the address sequence is sent, by an arbitration loss, by an address matched in slave mode, by a timeout error detection, or when PE = 0. If the I2C is already in master mode with AUTOEND = 0, setting this bit generates a Repeated Start condition when RELOAD=0, after the end of the NBYTES transfer. Otherwise setting this bit generates a START condition once the bus is free. Note: Writing '0' to this bit has no effect. Note: The START bit can be set even if the bus is BUSY or I2C is in slave mode. Note: This bit has no effect when RELOAD is set.

enum_values.name: NoStart
enum_values.description: No Start generation

enum_values.name: Start
enum_values.description: Restart/Start generation

enum_values.name: Start
enum_values.description: Restart/Start generation

fields.name: STOP
fields.description: Stop generation (master mode) The bit is set by software, cleared by hardware when a STOP condition is detected, or when PE = 0. In Master Mode: Note: Writing '0' to this bit has no effect.

enum_values.name: NoStop
enum_values.description: No Stop generation

enum_values.name: Stop
enum_values.description: Stop generation after current byte transfer

enum_values.name: Stop
enum_values.description: Stop generation after current byte transfer

fields.name: NACK
fields.description: NACK generation (slave mode) The bit is set by software, cleared by hardware when the NACK is sent, or when a STOP condition or an Address matched is received, or when PE = 0. Note: Writing '0' to this bit has no effect. Note: This bit is used in slave mode only: in master receiver mode, NACK is automatically generated after last byte preceding STOP or RESTART condition, whatever the NACK bit value. Note: When an overrun occurs in slave receiver NOSTRETCH mode, a NACK is automatically generated whatever the NACK bit value. Note: When hardware PEC checking is enabled (PECBYTE=1), the PEC acknowledge value does not depend on the NACK value.

enum_values.name: Ack
enum_values.description: an ACK is sent after current received byte

enum_values.name: Nack
enum_values.description: a NACK is sent after current received byte

enum_values.name: Nack
enum_values.description: a NACK is sent after current received byte

fields.name: NBYTES
fields.description: Number of bytes The number of bytes to be transmitted/received is programmed there. This field is don't care in slave mode with SBC=0. Note: Changing these bits when the START bit is set is not allowed.

fields.name: RELOAD
fields.description: NBYTES reload mode This bit is set and cleared by software.

enum_values.name: Completed
enum_values.description: The transfer is completed after the NBYTES data transfer (STOP or RESTART will follow)

enum_values.name: NotCompleted
enum_values.description: The transfer is not completed after the NBYTES data transfer (NBYTES will be reloaded)

fields.name: AUTOEND
fields.description: Automatic end mode (master mode) This bit is set and cleared by software. Note: This bit has no effect in slave mode or when the RELOAD bit is set.

enum_values.name: Software
enum_values.description: Software end mode: TC flag is set when NBYTES data are transferred, stretching SCL low

enum_values.name: Automatic
enum_values.description: Automatic end mode: a STOP condition is automatically sent when NBYTES data are transferred

fields.name: PECBYTE
fields.description: Packet error checking byte This bit is set by software, and cleared by hardware when the PEC is transferred, or when a STOP condition or an Address matched is received, also when PE = 0. Note: Writing '0' to this bit has no effect. Note: This bit has no effect when RELOAD is set. Note: This bit has no effect is slave mode when SBC=0. Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: NoPec
enum_values.description: No PEC transfer

enum_values.name: Pec
enum_values.description: PEC transmission/reception is requested

enum_values.name: Pec
enum_values.description: PEC transmission/reception is requested

fields.name: OA1
fields.description: Interface own slave address 7-bit addressing mode: OA1[7:1] contains the 7-bit own slave address. The bits OA1[9], OA1[8] and OA1[0] are don't care. 10-bit addressing mode: OA1[9:0] contains the 10-bit own slave address. Note: These bits can be written only when OA1EN=0.

fields.name: OA1MODE
fields.description: Own Address 1 10-bit mode Note: This bit can be written only when OA1EN=0.

enum_values.name: Bit7
enum_values.description: Own address 1 is a 7-bit address

enum_values.name: Bit10
enum_values.description: Own address 1 is a 10-bit address

fields.name: OA1EN
fields.description: Own Address 1 enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Own address 1 disabled. The received slave address OA1 is NACKed

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Own address 1 enabled. The received slave address OA1 is ACKed

fields.name: OA2
fields.description: Interface address 7-bit addressing mode: 7-bit address Note: These bits can be written only when OA2EN=0.

fields.name: OA2MSK
fields.description: Own Address 2 masks Note: These bits can be written only when OA2EN=0. Note: As soon as OA2MSK is not equal to 0, the reserved I2C addresses (0b0000xxx and 0b1111xxx) are not acknowledged even if the comparison matches.

enum_values.name: NoMask
enum_values.description: No mask

enum_values.name: Mask1
enum_values.description: OA2[1] is masked and don’t care. Only OA2[7:2] are compared

enum_values.name: Mask2
enum_values.description: OA2[2:1] are masked and don’t care. Only OA2[7:3] are compared

enum_values.name: Mask3
enum_values.description: OA2[3:1] are masked and don’t care. Only OA2[7:4] are compared

enum_values.name: Mask4
enum_values.description: OA2[4:1] are masked and don’t care. Only OA2[7:5] are compared

enum_values.name: Mask5
enum_values.description: OA2[5:1] are masked and don’t care. Only OA2[7:6] are compared

enum_values.name: Mask6
enum_values.description: OA2[6:1] are masked and don’t care. Only OA2[7] is compared.

enum_values.name: Mask7
enum_values.description: OA2[7:1] are masked and don’t care. No comparison is done, and all (except reserved) 7-bit received addresses are acknowledged

fields.name: OA2EN
fields.description: Own Address 2 enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Own address 2 disabled. The received slave address OA2 is NACKed

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Own address 2 enabled. The received slave address OA2 is ACKed

fields.name: SCLL
fields.description: SCL low period (master mode) This field is used to generate the SCL low period in master mode. tsubSCLL /sub= (SCLL+1) x tsubPRESC/sub Note: SCLL is also used to generate tsubBUF /suband tsubSU:STA /subtimings.

fields.name: SCLH
fields.description: SCL high period (master mode) This field is used to generate the SCL high period in master mode. tsubSCLH /sub= (SCLH+1) x tsubPRESC/sub Note: SCLH is also used to generate tsubSU:STO /suband tsubHD:STA /subtiming.

fields.name: SDADEL
fields.description: Data hold time This field is used to generate the delay tsubSDADEL /subbetween SCL falling edge and SDA edge. In master mode and in slave mode with NOSTRETCH = 0, the SCL line is stretched low during tsubSDADEL/sub. tsubSDADEL/sub= SDADEL x tsubPRESC/sub Note: SDADEL is used to generate tsubHD:DAT /subtiming.

fields.name: SCLDEL
fields.description: Data setup time This field is used to generate a delay tsubSCLDEL /subbetween SDA edge and SCL rising edge. In master mode and in slave mode with NOSTRETCH = 0, the SCL line is stretched low during tsubSCLDEL/sub. tsubSCLDEL /sub= (SCLDEL+1) x tsubPRESC/sub Note: tsubSCLDEL/sub is used to generate tsubSU:DAT /subtiming.

fields.name: PRESC
fields.description: Timing prescaler This field is used to prescale i2c_ker_ck in order to generate the clock period tsubPRESC /subused for data setup and hold counters (refer to FMPI2C timings on page 1928) and for SCL high and low level counters (refer to FMPI2C master initialization on page 1951). tsubPRESC /sub= (PRESC+1) x tsubI2CCLK/sub

fields.name: TIMEOUTA
fields.description: Bus Timeout A This field is used to configure: The SCL low timeout condition tsubTIMEOUT/sub when TIDLE=0 tsubTIMEOUT/sub= (TIMEOUTA+1) x 2048 x tsubI2CCLK/sub The bus idle condition (both SCL and SDA high) when TIDLE=1 tsubIDLE/sub= (TIMEOUTA+1) x 4 x tsubI2CCLK/sub Note: These bits can be written only when TIMOUTEN=0.

fields.name: TIDLE
fields.description: Idle clock timeout detection Note: This bit can be written only when TIMOUTEN=0.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: TIMEOUTA is used to detect SCL low timeout

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: TIMEOUTA is used to detect both SCL and SDA high timeout (bus idle condition)

fields.name: TIMOUTEN
fields.description: Clock timeout enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: SCL timeout detection is disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: SCL timeout detection is enabled

fields.name: TIMEOUTB
fields.description: Bus timeout B This field is used to configure the cumulative clock extension timeout: In master mode, the master cumulative clock low extend time (tsubLOW:MEXT/sub) is detected In slave mode, the slave cumulative clock low extend time (tsubLOW:SEXT/sub) is detected tsubLOW:EXT/sub= (TIMEOUTB+1) x 2048 x tsubI2CCLK/sub Note: These bits can be written only when TEXTEN=0.

fields.name: TEXTEN
fields.description: Extended clock timeout enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Extended clock timeout detection is disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Extended clock timeout detection is enabled

fields.name: TXE
fields.description: Transmit data register empty (transmitters) This bit is set by hardware when the I2C_TXDR register is empty. It is cleared when the next data to be sent is written in the I2C_TXDR register. This bit can be written to '1' by software in order to flush the transmit data register I2C_TXDR. Note: This bit is set by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NotEmpty
enum_values.description: TXDR register not empty

enum_values.name: Empty
enum_values.description: TXDR register empty

enum_values.name: Flush
enum_values.description: Flush the transmit data register

fields.name: TXIS
fields.description: Transmit interrupt status (transmitters) This bit is set by hardware when the I2C_TXDR register is empty and the data to be transmitted must be written in the I2C_TXDR register. It is cleared when the next data to be sent is written in the I2C_TXDR register. This bit can be written to '1' by software when NOSTRETCH = 1 only, in order to generate a TXIS event (interrupt if TXIE=1 or DMA request if TXDMAEN = 1). Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NotEmpty
enum_values.description: The TXDR register is not empty

enum_values.name: Empty
enum_values.description: The TXDR register is empty and the data to be transmitted must be written in the TXDR register

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: Generate a TXIS event

fields.name: RXNE
fields.description: Receive data register not empty (receivers) This bit is set by hardware when the received data is copied into the I2C_RXDR register, and is ready to be read. It is cleared when I2C_RXDR is read. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: Empty
enum_values.description: The RXDR register is empty

enum_values.name: NotEmpty
enum_values.description: Received data is copied into the RXDR register, and is ready to be read

fields.name: ADDR
fields.description: Address matched (slave mode) This bit is set by hardware as soon as the received slave address matched with one of the enabled slave addresses. It is cleared by software by setting ADDRCF bit. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NotMatch
enum_values.description: Adress mismatched or not received

enum_values.name: Match
enum_values.description: Received slave address matched with one of the enabled slave addresses

fields.name: NACKF
fields.description: Not Acknowledge received flag This flag is set by hardware when a NACK is received after a byte transmission. It is cleared by software by setting the NACKCF bit. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NoNack
enum_values.description: No NACK has been received

enum_values.name: Nack
enum_values.description: NACK has been received

fields.name: STOPF
fields.description: Stop detection flag This flag is set by hardware when a STOP condition is detected on the bus and the peripheral is involved in this transfer: either as a master, provided that the STOP condition is generated by the peripheral. or as a slave, provided that the peripheral has been addressed previously during this transfer. It is cleared by software by setting the STOPCF bit. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NoStop
enum_values.description: No Stop condition detected

enum_values.name: Stop
enum_values.description: Stop condition detected

fields.name: TC
fields.description: Transfer Complete (master mode) This flag is set by hardware when RELOAD=0, AUTOEND=0 and NBYTES data have been transferred. It is cleared by software when START bit or STOP bit is set. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NotComplete
enum_values.description: Transfer is not complete

enum_values.name: Complete
enum_values.description: NBYTES has been transfered

fields.name: TCR
fields.description: Transfer Complete Reload This flag is set by hardware when RELOAD=1 and NBYTES data have been transferred. It is cleared by software when NBYTES is written to a non-zero value. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0. Note: This flag is only for master mode, or for slave mode when the SBC bit is set.

enum_values.name: NotComplete
enum_values.description: Transfer is not complete

enum_values.name: Complete
enum_values.description: NBYTES has been transfered

fields.name: BERR
fields.description: Bus error This flag is set by hardware when a misplaced Start or STOP condition is detected whereas the peripheral is involved in the transfer. The flag is not set during the address phase in slave mode. It is cleared by software by setting BERRCF bit. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NoError
enum_values.description: No bus error

enum_values.name: Error
enum_values.description: Misplaced Start and Stop condition is detected

fields.name: ARLO
fields.description: Arbitration lost This flag is set by hardware in case of arbitration loss. It is cleared by software by setting the ARLOCF bit. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NotLost
enum_values.description: No arbitration lost

enum_values.name: Lost
enum_values.description: Arbitration lost

fields.name: OVR
fields.description: Overrun/Underrun (slave mode) This flag is set by hardware in slave mode with NOSTRETCH = 1, when an overrun/underrun error occurs. It is cleared by software by setting the OVRCF bit. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NoOverrun
enum_values.description: No overrun/underrun error occurs

enum_values.name: Overrun
enum_values.description: slave mode with NOSTRETCH=1, when an overrun/underrun error occurs

fields.name: PECERR
fields.description: PEC Error in reception This flag is set by hardware when the received PEC does not match with the PEC register content. A NACK is automatically sent after the wrong PEC reception. It is cleared by software by setting the PECCF bit. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0. Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: Match
enum_values.description: Received PEC does match with PEC register

enum_values.name: NoMatch
enum_values.description: Received PEC does not match with PEC register

fields.name: TIMEOUT
fields.description: Timeout or tsubLOW/sub detection flag This flag is set by hardware when a timeout or extended clock timeout occurred. It is cleared by software by setting the TIMEOUTCF bit. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0. Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: NoTimeout
enum_values.description: No timeout occured

enum_values.name: Timeout
enum_values.description: Timeout occured

fields.name: ALERT
fields.description: SMBus alert This flag is set by hardware when SMBHEN=1 (SMBus host configuration), ALERTEN=1 and a SMBALERT event (falling edge) is detected on SMBA pin. It is cleared by software by setting the ALERTCF bit. Note: This bit is cleared by hardware when PE = 0. Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: NoAlert
enum_values.description: SMBA alert is not detected

enum_values.name: Alert
enum_values.description: SMBA alert event is detected on SMBA pin

fields.name: BUSY
fields.description: Bus busy This flag indicates that a communication is in progress on the bus. It is set by hardware when a START condition is detected. It is cleared by hardware when a STOP condition is detected, or when PE = 0.

enum_values.name: NotBusy
enum_values.description: No communication is in progress on the bus

enum_values.name: Busy
enum_values.description: A communication is in progress on the bus

fields.name: DIR
fields.description: Transfer direction (Slave mode) This flag is updated when an address match event occurs (ADDR = 1).

enum_values.name: Write
enum_values.description: Write transfer, slave enters receiver mode

enum_values.name: Read
enum_values.description: Read transfer, slave enters transmitter mode

fields.name: ADDCODE
fields.description: Address match code (Slave mode) These bits are updated with the received address when an address match event occurs (ADDR = 1). In the case of a 10-bit address, ADDCODE provides the 10-bit header followed by the 2 MSBs of the address.

fields.name: ADDRCF
fields.description: Address matched flag clear Writing 1 to this bit clears the ADDR flag in the I2C_ISR register. Writing 1 to this bit also clears the START bit in the I2C_CR2 register.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clears the ADDR flag in ISR register

fields.name: NACKCF
fields.description: Not Acknowledge flag clear Writing 1 to this bit clears the NACKF flag in I2C_ISR register.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clears the NACK flag in ISR register

fields.name: STOPCF
fields.description: STOP detection flag clear Writing 1 to this bit clears the STOPF flag in the I2C_ISR register.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clears the STOP flag in ISR register

fields.name: BERRCF
fields.description: Bus error flag clear Writing 1 to this bit clears the BERRF flag in the I2C_ISR register.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clears the BERR flag in ISR register

fields.name: ARLOCF
fields.description: Arbitration lost flag clear Writing 1 to this bit clears the ARLO flag in the I2C_ISR register.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clears the ARLO flag in ISR register

fields.name: OVRCF
fields.description: Overrun/Underrun flag clear Writing 1 to this bit clears the OVR flag in the I2C_ISR register.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clears the OVR flag in ISR register

fields.name: PECCF
fields.description: PEC Error flag clear Writing 1 to this bit clears the PECERR flag in the I2C_ISR register. Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clears the PEC flag in ISR register

fields.name: TIMOUTCF
fields.description: Timeout detection flag clear Writing 1 to this bit clears the TIMEOUT flag in the I2C_ISR register. Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clears the TIMOUT flag in ISR register

fields.name: ALERTCF
fields.description: Alert flag clear Writing 1 to this bit clears the ALERT flag in the I2C_ISR register. Note: If the SMBus feature is not supported, this bit is reserved and forced by hardware to '0'. Refer to Section 52.3: FMPI2C implementation.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Clears the ALERT flag in ISR register

fields.name: PEC
fields.description: Packet error checking register This field contains the internal PEC when PECEN=1. The PEC is cleared by hardware when PE = 0.

fields.name: RXDATA
fields.description: 8-bit receive data Data byte received from the Isup2/supC bus

fields.name: TXDATA
fields.description: 8-bit transmit data Data byte to be transmitted to the Isup2/supC bus Note: These bits can be written only when TXE = 1.

fields.name: TCDMAEN
fields.description: DMA request enable on Transfer Complete event

fields.name: TCRDMAEN
fields.description: DMA request enable on Transfer Complete Reload event

fields.name: TRIGSEL
fields.description: Trigger selection (refer to Section 52.4.3: FMPI2C pins and internal signals I2C interconnections tables). ... Note: This bit can be written only when PE = 0

fields.name: TRIGPOL
fields.description: Trigger polarity Note: This bit can be written only when PE = 0

fields.name: TRIGEN
fields.description: Trigger enable When a trigger is detected, a START condition is sent and the transfer is launched as defined in I2C_CR2.
